pairs.question: What was the date of the 2012 general elections in Puerto Rico, and which officials were elected?
pairs.answer: The general elections in Puerto Rico were held on Tuesday, November 6, 2012. Voters elected the governor, resident commissioner, and members of the Legislative Assembly for a four-year term.

pairs.question: Who won the 2012 gubernatorial election in Puerto Rico, and which party did they represent?
pairs.answer: Alejandro García Padilla from the Popular Democratic Party (PPD) won the 2012 gubernatorial election in Puerto Rico.

pairs.question: How many parties participated in the 2012 Puerto Rican general elections, and how does this compare to previous elections?
pairs.answer: Six parties participated in the 2012 Puerto Rican general elections. This was the second time in more than 40 years that six parties participated.

pairs.question: What historic firsts occurred during the 2012 Puerto Rican elections?
pairs.answer: The 2012 elections marked the first time in more than 60 years that a status referendum was held on the same day as the general election, and the first time in Puerto Rico that absentee ballots were issued for citizens out of the country on election day.

pairs.question: Who was the incumbent governor defeated in the 2012 election, and which party did they represent?
pairs.answer: Luis Fortuño from the New Progressive Party (PNP) was the incumbent governor defeated in the 2012 election.

pairs.question: What was the margin of victory for Pedro Pierluisi in the 2012 Resident Commissioner election?
pairs.answer: Pedro Pierluisi won the 2012 Resident Commissioner election by a margin of almost 1.3% over his closest opponent, Rafael Cox Alomar.

pairs.question: As of 2026, when was the last time a member of the Popular Democratic Party won the governorship of Puerto Rico?
pairs.answer: As of 2026, the last time a member of the Popular Democratic Party won the governorship of Puerto Rico was in the 2012 election, when Alejandro García Padilla was elected.
